decks.notes: This Eldrazi Aggro deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with powerful Eldrazi creatures and disrupting their game plan with cards like Thought-Knot Seer and Chalice of the Void. By utilizing mana acceleration with Lotus Petal and Ancient Tomb, the deck aims to play big threats early in the game and close out the match quickly. The deck also takes advantage of Eldrazi Temple and Eye of Ugin to further accelerate its mana and cast its Eldrazi creatures more efficiently. With its aggressive gameplay and disruptive elements, this deck is designed to apply constant pressure on the opponent.

The deck focuses on applying early pressure with efficient creatures and disruption spells to control the game tempo. It aims to quickly build a board presence while disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Fatal Push, Thoughtseize, and Daze. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides card advantage and versatility, while Murktide Regent and Brazen Borrower provide additional threats. The mana base is optimized for consistency and includes some utility lands like Wasteland and Undercity Sewers. Overall, the deck aims to stay ahead of the opponent while maintaining control of the battlefield.

The main concept of this Mono Black Combo Legacy deck is to utilize fast mana and card advantage to quickly assemble a winning combo. By using cards like Dark Ritual, Lion's Eye Diamond, and Burning Wish, the deck aims to generate a large amount of mana and card advantage to either win the game with cards like Tendrils of Agony or create a board state that is difficult for the opponent to come back from. The deck also features some graveyard recursion with cards like Echo of Eons and Gaea's Will to keep the combo going.

This deck aims to quickly cheat out powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Atraxa, Grand Unifier with cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack, allowing for explosive plays that can overwhelm the opponent. The inclusion of counterspells like Force of Will and Flusterstorm help protect the combo pieces, while card draw spells like Brainstorm and Ponder help find the necessary pieces to execute the combo. The deck also includes versatile cards like Brazen Borrower and Izzet Charm to provide additional utility in different situations.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on building a strong board presence with low-cost creatures like Giver of Runes and Ocelot Pride, supported by powerful anthems from cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory. It also includes disruptive elements such as Hexing Squelcher and Static Prison to control the opponent's game plan. The deck utilizes efficient removal like Swords to Plowshares and synergy with Goblin Bombardment. The mana base is carefully constructed with a mix of dual lands and fetch lands to ensure consistent access to the deck's spells.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's board and resources while ramping up mana production through Gaea's Cradle and utilizing powerful green creatures to overwhelm the opponent. It uses powerful cards like Thoughtseize and Natural Order to disrupt the opponent's gameplan and find key pieces such as Craterhoof Behemoth for a game-ending finish. The deck also includes hate cards like Collector Ouphe to shut down artifact-based strategies and utilizes creatures like Endurance and Springheart Nantuko for graveyard hate and disruption. The goal is to establish a dominant board presence and overpower the opponent with large creatures.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of lands and spells. It utilizes cards like Crop Rotation, Mox Diamond, and Life from the Loam to quickly assemble powerful land combinations. The main win condition is the Dark Depths/Thespian's Stage combo to create a 20/20 Marit Lage token. Punishing Fire provides removal and recurring damage, while Urza's Saga can tutor for key artifacts. The deck also includes utility lands like Karakas and Maze of Ith for protection. Overall, the strategy revolves around land manipulation and resource denial to outvalue and outlast the opponent.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with creatures and utilizing various fast mana sources to cast a series of spells in order to win the game with a combo finisher. The goal is to play a high number of spells in a single turn to trigger multiple powerful effects and win the game in a single explosive turn. The deck relies on using graveyard recursion, free mana sources, and card draw to consistently assemble the combo pieces and execute the win condition efficiently.

The main concept of this Boros Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and powerful spells. The deck focuses on early game board presence with cards like Sand Scout and Ocelot Pride, while also utilizing removal spells like Swords to Plowshares to deal with opposing threats. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory provide additional support and buffs to the creatures, while Static Prison and Hexing Squelcher help control the opponent's board. The deck also includes a mix of lands to ensure consistent mana production and spell casting.

The deck focuses on utilizing mana acceleration and creature toolbox cards to control the board and ramp into powerful threats like Craterhoof Behemoth. By utilizing cards like Green Sun's Zenith and Natural Order, the deck can tutor for specific creatures to fit the situation. The inclusion of cards like Endurance and Collector Ouphe help disrupt opponent's strategies while cards like Sylvan Safekeeper and Wight of the Reliquary provide utility and resilience. The deck aims to control the game through efficient creature deployment and resource denial while setting up for powerful finishers.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan and controlling the board while quickly deploying threats to close out the game. Using a combination of hand disruption spells, counterspells, and removal, it aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while establishing a strong board presence. The deck's strategy revolves around efficiently deploying threats like Murktide Regent and Orcish Bowmasters to apply pressure on the opponent and force them to react. The deck's mana base is designed to support both its aggressive plays and disruptive elements, ensuring it can adapt to various matchups effectively.

The deck aims to control the game through disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Force of Will while deploying efficient threats like Murktide Regent and Nethergoyf. It utilizes tempo plays and card advantage from cards like Daze and Brainstorm to maintain a fast pace and pressure the opponent. The mana base is designed to support a proactive game plan, with access to both blue and black sources to cast the deck's powerful spells. This deck seeks to apply pressure early and disrupt the opponent's game plan to secure a quick victory.

The deck focuses on disrupting and controlling the opponent while applying pressure through efficient creatures and spells. It aims to tempo out the opponent by utilizing disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Daze to protect its threats such as Murktide Regent and Nethergoyf. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides card advantage and selection, while Kaito, Bane of Nightmares offers a powerful finisher. The mana base is built to support both blue and black spells, with Wasteland providing additional disruption. Overall, this deck aims to outmaneuver and outplay the opponent in a fast-paced Legacy format.

This deck focuses on utilizing lands as a resource to control the game, with cards like Exploration and Life from the Loam to generate value. It aims to assemble the Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage combo to create a powerful Marit Lage token, while also disrupting the opponent with cards like Wasteland and The Tabernacle at Pendrell Vale. The deck includes utility lands like Karakas and Bojuka Bog to deal with specific threats, along with protection spells like Sejiri Steppe. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent through land manipulation and control elements.

The primary focus of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan through resource denial, hand disruption, and constant threat presence. By using cards like Smallpox, Thoughtseize, and Liliana of the Veil, the deck aims to control the board and limit the opponent's options. The inclusion of cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Expedition Map provides additional utility and flexibility in dealing with various threats. Overall, the deck combines control elements with land destruction and hand disruption to slowly grind down the opponent and achieve victory through attrition.

The deck focuses on cheating big, game-ending creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Atraxa, Grand Unifier into play using cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. By using card draw and manipulation like Brainstorm and Ponder, the deck aims to assemble the combo pieces quickly while using counterspells like Force of Will to protect its game plan. The key strategy is to ramp into powerful threats early on and overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to establish their own board presence.
